full_prompt: Design a quick-release payload mount bracket for a Group-2 tactical UAS per a representative defense-innovation open SBIR mechanical-structures brief: mount a 2.5 kg gimbal payload to the bottom of an airframe rail with a tool-less quarter-turn mechanism, survive +/- 9 g operational flight envelope and 6 m/s hard landing, and be 100% 3D-printable in field conditions (FDM PETG or LPBF AlSi10Mg). Envelope: 120 x 80 x 40 mm. Interfaces: airframe rail dovetail (MIL-STD-1913 compatible 21.2 mm width, 3.5 mm depth) on the top face; payload interface on bottom face with a 4-point Picatinny-adjacent pattern (M4 bolts on 50 x 50 mm square). Preferred material: LPBF AlSi10Mg (yield 230 MPa, UTS 345 MPa, E 68 GPa, density 2670 kg/m^3); alternate field material FDM PETG (yield 50 MPa, UTS 53 MPa, E 2.0 GPa, density 1270 kg/m^3). Load cases: LC1 +9 g vertical payload = 2.5 kg * 9 * 9.81 = 221 N; LC2 -9 g vertical = 221 N (inverted); LC3 +6 g lateral = 147 N; LC4 6 m/s hard landing with 50 ms half-sine shock = effective 30 g = 736 N; LC5 gust +5 g combined with +3 g lateral (vector sum magnitude ~143 N). Pass/fail under DoD SBIR flight-hardware guidance, SF 1.5 on ultimate for non-man-rated UAS structures: (R1) max von Mises in AlSi10Mg primary material <= 345 / 1.5 = 230 MPa under all LC; (R2) max von Mises in PETG field-printed alternate <= 53 / 2.0 = 26.5 MPa (higher SF 2.0 for FDM process variability); (R3) payload tip deflection under LC1 <= 1.5 mm for gimbal optical axis stability; (R4) quarter-turn release mechanism retention force >= 500 N (5x payload weight at 1g for safe handling); (R5) mass <= 150 g AlSi10Mg / <= 90 g PETG; (R6) print time target <= 3 h FDM PETG (enables field replacement); (R7) dovetail engagement contact stress <= 150 MPa AlSi10Mg / 40 MPa PETG (prevent rail wear or bracket yielding). Deliverables: STEP AP242 + 3MF for both material variants; report with per-case max stress, deflection, retention force, mass, and print-time estimate.
short_prompt:
Design a quick-release payload mount bracket for a Group-2 tactical UAS: mount a 2.5 kg gimbal payload to the bottom of an airframe rail with a tool-less quarter-turn mechanism, survive +/- 9 g operational flight envelope and 6 m/s hard landing, and be 100% 3D-printable in field conditions (FDM PETG or LPBF AlSi10Mg).

Envelope: 120 x 80 x 40 mm. Interfaces: MIL-STD-1913 compatible dovetail (21.2 mm width, 3.5 mm depth) on the top face; bottom face 4-point Picatinny-adjacent pattern (M4 bolts on 50 x 50 mm square).

Materials: LPBF AlSi10Mg primary; alternate field FDM PETG.

Design SF 1.5 on ultimate for AlSi10Mg, 2.0 for FDM PETG (per DoD SBIR flight-hardware guidance for non-man-rated UAS).

Load cases.
- LC1 +9 g vertical: 2.5 kg x 9 x 9.81 = 221 N.
- LC2 -9 g vertical: 221 N inverted.
- LC3 +6 g lateral: 147 N.
- LC4 6 m/s hard landing, 50 ms half-sine shock, effective 30 g = 736 N.
- LC5 gust +5 g combined +3 g lateral (vector-sum ~143 N).

Gates include payload-tip deflection under LC1 <= 1.5 mm, quarter-turn release retention >= 500 N, mass <= 150 g AlSi10Mg / <= 90 g PETG, print-time <= 3 h FDM PETG, dovetail contact <= 150 MPa AlSi10Mg / 40 MPa PETG.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Print time is a slicer output, not FEA.
Hard-landing shock SRS may be used instead of transient FEA.

verification.requires_shock_note: LC4 transient shock may be run as a full transient or as SRS-equivalent quasi-static at 30 g.

prompt.title: Quick-release Group-2 UAS payload mount (defense-innovation SBIR template)
prompt.brief: Design a 120x80x40 mm quick-release payload mount carrying a 2.5 kg gimbal payload through +/-9 g flight and 30 g landing shock, printable in AlSi10Mg or PETG.

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: 5x payload weight at 1g for handling safety
prevent rail wear or bracket yielding

requirements.governing_standard: DoD SBIR flight-hardware guidance + MIL-STD-1913 (rail) + DoD UAS flight envelope typical

analysis_types: linear static x5 + transient shock or SRS for LC4

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Operational temp range covered by material data; no gradient analysis.

notes.numeric_source: Typical defense-innovation open-topic envelopes for Group-2 UAS secondary structure (no specific topic ID used); +/-9 g is DoD tactical UAS standard envelope.
